title: Mammillaria densispina
taxonomy: Mammillaria densispina is classified within the plant family Cactaceae. This species is further grouped into the subfamily Mammilloideae and the tribe Pachycereeae. Mammillaria densispina is part of the Mammillaria genus, which is a diverse group of small to medium-sized cacti. The exact classification may vary depending on the source or recent taxonomic revisions. Within the Mammillaria genus, Mammillaria densispina is often distinguished by its unique appearance and growth habits.

side_effects: Mammillaria densispina is a type of cactus, and like many cacti, it's not typically known for causing significant side effects in humans. intro: Mammillaria densispina, also known as the "pine cone cactus," is a species of cactus that belongs to the Echinocereus genus. The health benefits of Mammillaria densispina include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to help soothe skin irritations, wounds, and infections.
